title: Ordered collections of item-refs need a convergent sequence field-kind — an array index cannot merge concurrent insert / delete / move
description: Order is a plain ordered array of refs on the owning item (e.g. a fragment_refs / phase-order / step-list field), authoritative by design. Single-writer insert/reorder works: splice the array, one atomic item write. But the array carries no merge semantics: two divergent versions that each reorder it 3-way-merge to a conflict (git) or whole-array last-writer-wins (item upsert), losing one side's ordering. The gap: when two divergent substrate versions each insert, delete, and/or move elements in the same ordered collection, the substrate must merge them into one deterministic, replica-independent sequence containing every insert, with each element retaining a single identity and a single position, and concurrently-inserted runs kept coherent rather than interleaved. An array index cannot carry this, because position is a value two branches generate independently and must reconcile. The capability is latent until branch-pinned substrates + a substrate merge driver (FGAP-004) exist.

evidence.reference: ordering today is a plain ordered array of refs on the owning item (authoritative by design); position is an array index two branches generate independently and cannot reconcile
item upsert is whole-array last-writer-wins and git line-merge mangles arrays; neither merges concurrent insert/delete/move on an ordered ref-array — one side's ordering is lost

impact: Ordered collections of item-refs (roadmaps, milestone sequences, fragment composition, step lists) in substrates branched and merged across operators / sessions / branches cannot converge: concurrent reordering on divergent versions conflicts or silently drops an ordering.
proposed_resolution: A declared sequence-field kind whose elements carry unique, densely-orderable, immutable position identifiers minted relative to neighbors at insert time (non-interleaving Fugue-class sequence CRDT); move as a first-class op via a position register resolved by hybrid logical clock (Kleppmann move-CRDT) with stable element identity; HLC for replica-independent same-gap ordering + move resolution; a field-aware merge driver composing the per-field CRDT op-sets/states; a sort-on-read projection emitting a plain ordered ref-array (and translating array-position hand-edits back to CRDT ops); authoritative CRDT state in the owning <block>.json, projection + merge driver in block-api. Plugs into the substrate merge driver (FGAP-004) as a field-kind. See DEC-0005.
